column1: # macros.tsv — every public macro in Lua headers → Rust equivalent
# Conventions: o = TValue, t = Table, ts = TString, ci = CallInfo, L = lua_State, g = global_State, n = Node.
# Translate call-site form, not the macro body. Types follow PORTING.md §3 (LuaValue/LuaState/etc.).

column4: C-side `extern` for the public API; emit `pub` at the Rust declaration site
auxiliary-library visibility; identical to `pub` in Rust
stdlib module entry-point visibility; emit `pub` at decl site
internal-but-cross-file in C; in Rust crate-private suffices
used for declarations of global tables (e.g. `luaP_opmodes`); emit `pub(crate) static`
empty in C build; drop entirely — Rust uses the definition site directly
stack hard limit; emit as `const` in `lua_vm`
alignment hack for `Udata`/`UValue`; drop — Rust handles alignment via `#[repr(C)]`+fields
noreturn fns; Rust returns `Result<...>`-with-`Err` everywhere, so usually becomes a plain return type. Only `l_noret` fns that genuinely diverge use `!`
attribute, not visibility; place above the fn
`static inline` collapses to a module-private `#[inline] fn`
sentinel for "no value at this stack index"; only meaningful in stack-query helpers
covers both Int and Float variants

LUA_TFUNCTION	lua.h	LuaType::Function	covers Lclosure / Cclosure / lcf
LUA_TUSERDATA	lua.h	LuaType::UserData

LUA_NUMTYPES	lua.h	const LUA_NUMTYPES: usize = 9	number of base types; used to size metatable arrays
LUA_TUPVAL	lobject.h	LuaType::UpVal	internal-only collectable type
LUA_TPROTO	lobject.h	LuaType::Proto	internal-only collectable type
LUA_TDEADKEY	lobject.h	LuaType::DeadKey	tombstone for removed table keys; surfaces via `keyisdead`
LUA_TOTALTYPES	lobject.h	const LUA_TOTALTYPES: usize = LUA_TPROTO as usize + 2	used to size `luaT_typenames_`
LUA_VNIL	lobject.h	LuaValue::Nil	standard" nil; the only nil variant we materialize in Rust

upvalues aren't a LuaValue variant either

for table-slot checks; in Rust table API returns `Option`

gcvalueraw	lobject.h	v.gc	C-union accessor; drop in Rust
tsvalue	lobject.h	o.as_string().expect(not string")
C marks slot as VEMPTY; Rust uses Nil and table API distinguishes via `Option`
change-only variant; C asserts existing variant. In Rust just reassign.

setsvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Str(x.clone())	x: GcRef<LuaString>
sethvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Table(x.clone())	x: GcRef<LuaTable>
setuvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::UserData(x.clone())	x: GcRef<LuaUserData>
setpvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::LightUserData(x)	x: *mut c_void
setthvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Thread(x.clone())	x: GcRef<LuaState>
setclLvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(x.clone()))	x: GcRef of L-closure
setclCvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::C(x.clone()))	x: GcRef of C-closure
setfvalue	lobject.h	*o = LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::LightC(x))	x: lua_CFunction (fn pointer)
setgcovalue	lstate.h	*o = (&x).to_lua_value()	C dispatches on GCObject->tt; in Rust the conversion is by variant
setobj	lobject.h	*obj1 = obj2.clone()	plain TValue copy; LuaValue: Clone is cheap (refcount bump for GC variants)
setnodekey	lobject.h	n.set_key(state, obj)	copies a TValue into a Node's key fields
getnodekey	lobject.h	*obj = n.key_value()	copies a Node's key out as a TValue

# --- Stack value setters ---
s2v	lobject.h	state.stack_at(idx)	convert StackValue -> &TValue; in Rust the stack holds LuaValue directly
setobjs2s	lobject.h	state.set_at(o1, state.get_at(o2).clone())	stack-to-stack copy; clone first to avoid borrow conflict
setobj2s	lobject.h	state.set_at(o1, v.clone())	external-value-to-stack copy
setobj2n	lobject.h	*dst = src.clone()	alias of setobj; to new object"
